Code fragment: public final TableField<ParentsRecord, ParentTypeDto> PARENT_TYPE = createField(DSL.name("parent_type"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(10).nullable(false), this, "Отец или мать", new EnumConverter<String, ParentTypeDto>(String.class, ParentTypeDto.class));
Explanation: The PARENT_TYPE column uses an EnumConverter to restrict values to a fixed set (father/mother), which embeds business logic in the column definition. Consider creating a separate lookup table for parent types to allow easier modification of allowed values without schema changes.

Code fragment: public final TableField<PersonsRecord, UUID> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.UUID.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("gen_random_uuid()"), SQLDataType.UUID)), this, "Идентификатор человека");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'person_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'person_id' to make foreign key relationships more self-documenting and to avoid ambiguity in queries with multiple joined tables.

Using selectFrom(PERSONS) fetches all columns from the PERSONS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and reduce performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed for PersonWithRelativesDto, e.g., dsl.select(PERSONS.ID, PERSONS.FIRST_NAME, ...).from(PERSONS).

The method `prefixAliasAllFieldsBy` selects all columns from the PERSONS table using `table.fieldStream()`, which fetches every column regardless of actual need. Explicitly list only the required columns in the select clause to improve query efficiency and maintainability.
